name: The Moon
description: The Moon is Earth's only natural satellite. It is the fifth largest satellite in the Solar System and the largest relative to the size of its parent planet, tidally locked to Earth.

name: Phobos
description: Phobos is the larger and closer of Mars' two moons. It is heavily cratered and has a lumpy, irregular shape. Phobos orbits Mars faster than Mars rotates, rising in the west and setting in the east.

name: Deimos
description: Deimos is the smaller and more distant moon of Mars. It is smoother in appearance than Phobos due to a thick layer of regolith dust filling its craters. It takes 30 hours to cross the sky.

name: Io
description: Io is the most volcanically active world in the Solar System, with hundreds of volcanoes erupting fountains of lava and sulfur, driven by tidal heating from Jupiter and neighbor moons.

name: Europa
description: Europa has a smooth, cracked water-ice crust covering a vast global subsurface liquid water ocean that contains more water than all of Earth's oceans combined, a prime target for life.

name: Ganymede
description: Ganymede is the largest moon in the Solar System, even larger than the planet Mercury. It is the only moon known to possess its own magnetosphere, with a salt-water ocean underneath.

name: Callisto
description: Callisto is the second-largest moon of Jupiter and the most heavily cratered object in the Solar System, showing almost no signs of geological activity since its formation.

name: Titan
description: Titan is Saturn's largest moon, the second-largest in the Solar System, and the only moon known to have a dense atmosphere and stable bodies of surface liquid (methane and ethane lakes).

name: Enceladus
description: Enceladus is a small, icy moon famous for its active geysers venting water vapor, ice particles, and organic compounds from a global subsurface ocean out of fractures at its south pole.

name: Ariel
description: Ariel is the brightest and youngest of Uranus's major moons. Its surface is scarred by deep rift valleys and flat plains, indicating a history of intense geological activity and ice volcanism.

name: Umbriel
description: Umbriel is the darkest and oldest of Uranus's major moons. Heavily cratered with a dark surface composition, its only bright feature is a mysterious glowing ring of ice inside Wunda crater.

name: Titania
description: Titania is the largest moon of Uranus. It is composed of equal parts ice and rock, and features massive canyon systems and scarps created by the expansion and cracking of its interior as it froze.

name: Oberon
description: Oberon is the outermost major moon of Uranus. It is heavily cratered with dark material coating the floors of many impact basins, showing signs of internal water-ice upwelling.

name: Triton
description: Triton is Neptune's largest moon. It is the only large moon in the Solar System that orbits in the opposite direction of its planet's rotation (retrograde orbit), suggesting it is a captured Kuiper Belt object.

name: Charon
description: Charon is Pluto's largest moon. It is so massive relative to Pluto that they orbit a common center of gravity outside Pluto's surface, effectively making them a binary dwarf planet system.
